title: Mildly Pungent Peruvian Kidney Bean Stew
description: A festive Peruvian stew with kidney beans, leftover pork, and a subtle heat from green chilies, cooked in a comal for a flaky finish.

ingredients.name: diced leftover pork
ingredients.notes: use leftover roast pork, shredded or diced

instructions: Heat the comal over medium heat and add the olive oil.
Sauté the diced onion until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and sliced green chili, cooking for another minute.
Stir in the cumin, paprika, and tomato paste until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Pour in the water or broth, bringing the mixture to a gentle boil.
Add the cooked kidney beans, diced leftover pork, and chopped spinach, reducing heat to low.
Simmer the stew for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the flavors meld and the spinach wilts.
Season with salt to taste, tasting and adjusting if necessary.
Turn off the heat and let the stew rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Garnish with fresh cilantro or a squeeze of lime if desired, then serve hot.
